subject: Social Science (CBSE - History & Civics)
chapter: Social and Political Life – II: Understanding Media

activities_projects.title: Comparative Newspaper Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how different newspapers report the same event
steps: Select one major news story covered in multiple newspapers
Compare headlines, language, and focus of each article
Write a short analysis identifying differences and possible reasons
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand media perspective and bias
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis and accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Investigate Fake News
steps: Find one news story or social media post that seems suspicious
Verify facts using credible sources
Write a brief report on your verification process and conclusion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn critical thinking and fact-checking skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Correctness and explanation of verification steps (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Media Representation Project
activities_projects.objective: Understand how media represents social issues
steps: Select one social issue (e.g., pollution, education, gender equality)
Collect three different articles covering the issue
Analyze how each article presents the problem and solution
Summarize your findings in a short report
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students critically analyze media framing of social issues
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of analysis and report clarity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Create a Digital Media Presentation
activities_projects.objective: Learn to present media content creatively
steps: Choose a news topic or event
Collect relevant media content (images, videos, news excerpts)
Prepare a 5–7 slide presentation highlighting key facts and different perspectives
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students enhance digital literacy and presentation skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Content quality, clarity, and creativity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Role-play Debate: Media Ethics
steps: Divide the class into journalists, editors, and audience
Present a scenario involving ethical dilemmas (e.g., sensationalism, privacy issues)
Debate possible solutions and responsibilities
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand media ethics and decision-making
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation, reasoning, and understanding of ethics (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Media Influence Study
materials: Newspaper, magazine, or social media posts
steps: Select one advertisement or news story aimed at influencing people
Identify techniques used to persuade or influence the audience
Write a short reflection on how it might affect public opinion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop critical awareness of media influence
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of analysis and reflection (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Interview with a Media Professional
steps: Prepare 5–7 questions about reporting, editing, and ethics
Interview a local journalist, radio host, or social media influencer
Write a summary highlighting insights gained
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn about media work firsthand
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of questions, interview summary, and insights (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Fact vs Opinion Chart
activities_projects.objective: Distinguish between factual reporting and opinions in media
steps: Select an article with both facts and opinions
Create a chart dividing statements into ‘Fact’ and ‘Opinion’
Discuss why distinguishing is important
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve media literacy and critical reading skills

activities_projects.title: Media Vocabulary Research
steps: Identify 15 new media-related terms (e.g., censorship, press release, editorial, propaganda)
Write definitions and examples for each
Share with classmates or create a glossary
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students enhance media-related vocabulary and understanding

activities_projects.title: Create a Media Awareness Campaign
materials: Chart paper, poster sheets, or digital tools
steps: Design a campaign to educate peers about verifying news and avoiding misinformation
Include slogans, visuals, and examples
Present the campaign to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn to communicate media awareness effectively
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, clarity, and effectiveness of campaign (10 marks)
